raw_consumer_statement:
Called what I thought was XXXX XXXX, on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX but it turned out to be an imposter who booked my travel on an affiliate airline. I was one digit off on phone number which I discovered within an hour of booking as it made no sense to be booked on XXXX XXXX and paid extra {$300.00} fee. My card is Citi AA. I called the number back and realized it was a spoof. The imposter used XXXX. XX/XX/XXXX is XXXX. 

I called Citi right away to shut down my card. They issued a credit for the {$300.00} promptly. I spoke with the security department and went through other reps through a series of calls to Citi on XX/XX/XXXX in between XXXX until XXXX. I was worried the imposter had my birthdates, address and credit card number. 

To my surprise, on review of my Citi online transactions, on XX/XX/XXXX after vacation, there was a reversal from Citi and charge back to me of the {$300.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. I had not received any request for information and recall Citi reps sympathetic approach and assurance I indeed was spoofed and the matter would be taken care of. 

I called Citi on XX/XX/XXXX and a rep said theyd try to reopen the case. 

This was an imposter acting as a flight reservation - the situation of a reversal decision was that Citi heard back from the imposter and believed them over my very detailed description of what had transpired. It does not make any sense to have explained the matter to Citi, of which I did upon receiving a confirmation from XXXX XXXX with an email of XXXX which was not mine!!! I knew I had been scammed. 

I am at the mercy of Citi and their security and investigation department which has received my complete detailed information yet they reversed the charge and believes the imposter over me because they said I gave the imposter my information. 

Let it be known I have a Citi XXXX XXXX card.
